title: gagea pygmaea subsp. iberica
intro: Gagea pygmaea subsp. iberica, also known as the Iberian lesser corydalis, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the lily family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system, although further research is necessary to confirm these claims. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, which are believed to be effective in treating a range of health issues, although the efficacy and safety of these preparations require further study.
